title: Dont you see 3i CAUSED 2i quantum magic fanwankery
display_title: Dont you see 3i CAUSED 2i quantum magic fanwankery
wikitext:
[]: Don't you see?! 3i CAUSED 2i! (quantum magic fanwankery)             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Thu Jan 22, 2009 11:33 am I'm attempting a unified theory of quantum magic in evangelion. Please poke holes. This is in here because it's blatant fanwank, and shouldn't be mistaken for anything anyone should take seriously or complain about. Okay. Let's assume that in the evangelion universe, we explain the bell effect by quantum non-locality (entangled particles interact across space and time via information-without-media, the standard cop-out for the complaint that entanglement works faster than light). Having the angels have the ability to manipulate matter via entanglement suddenly explains a lot of shit: 1) AT Fields can be explained by the manipulation of existing types of fields (electromagnetic fields being one) 2) The shapeshifting and the ability to 'make matter out of thin air' can be explained by regrouping existing particles through entanglement. 3) The whole light show during EoE seems less spectacular. Now, none of that actually violates the laws of physics. However, within our current notion of entanglement, we actually would need far more particles than could be inside the angel to get that level of control. With the number of particles within a given angel, it would really be pure luck that anything more than gross control could come from manipulating groups entangled together, and it could also totally destablize the angel if those particles were moved by other (mundane) forces. So, okay. Obviously, something is wrong here. But wait! With non-local entanglement, the particles can be remote controlled from arbitrary distances. Something across the universe can physically move about a scale model of earth on the particle level, and if all the particles are entangled properly with the particles here, they can control it. Not only that, but they themselves can remain totally invisible and unable to come to harm. Not only can they do this across distance, but they can also do it across time. What if the ancestral race is remote controlling earth from the past? What if Adam and Lillith were sent to entangle earth with a remote model of itself? The most solid argument for the anthropic principle, according to Robert Anton Wilson, is the idea that the universe is made for humans by humans, due to a feedback loop in time. By doing the kinds of quantum mechanics experiments that we do, we modify the entangled partners in the past of the particles we experiment on in the present and future. We simultaneously fine-tune ourselves and our universe by modifying our universe's beginnings by trying to figure them out. We create the universe by discovering it. SEELE's prophecy has to have come from somewhere. Obviously, third impact created a great wave of reactions back in time. Third impact changed the world, not only in its future, but also in its past. Third impact caused itself to become impossible to prevent. Third impact caused second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jan 22, 2009 11:38 am Farfetched theory is faaaaaaarfetched. You might also want to double-check your quantum mechanics.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Thu Jan 22, 2009 11:42 am Farfetched theories that are farfetched are the best kind! And goddess forbid any of our fanwanking actually connect in any way with *reality*. Lol, reality...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 22, 2009 1:36 pm Last I checked quantum entanglement disregards space but happens Instantaneously... not in the future or past. More fun is the idea that a time-displaced Rei-tachi caused 2I. Whee, paradox for everyone!

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Thu Jan 22, 2009 1:44 pm I'm pretty sure that non-locality (action at a distance) disregards time as well. It's kind of necessary in order to disregard space. They're kind of the same thing on that level of abstraction anyway. I see no reason why something that applies to space can't apply to time, but I'm pretty sure the canon interpretation of action at a distance includes over time. That said, it's a fanwank, and should not be in any way tied down to reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jan 22, 2009 6:35 pm You know, everybody on this forum thinks time is linear--a direct cause-to-effect relationship, when actually it's more like a...great big ball of...wibbly-wobbly, timey-wimey...stuff, not unlike the Angel that captured Shinji within its shadow.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Jan 22, 2009 10:08 pm Well I like it ^__^ Fun read...Now if that is true then that explains the things in Rebuild...Hmmm..jkjk

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 23, 2009 12:01 am Enki v.2 wrote: I'm pretty sure that non-locality (action at a distance) disregards time as well. It's kind of necessary in order to disregard space.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri Jan 23, 2009 11:47 am I would have to agree with NemZ. One might posit another theory to include travel across the time dimension, but as I understand it (and the way it might be languaged might be that) current theory merely removes time as a constraining dimension (things happen simultaneously because they are "entangled" as a single object) rather than presenting time as a coordinate system that can be navigated. One would need to attach a new level of understanding to current theory to have this make "sense" (which would be delightful). edit: typo

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sat Jan 24, 2009 7:48 pm NemZ wrote: Enki v.2 wrote: I'm pretty sure that non-locality (action at a distance) disregards time as well. It's kind of necessary in order to disregard space.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 24, 2009 10:43 pm Well, as long as we're passing around one massive physics-related epic fanwankage, I might as well toss this one here too instead of making a new thread. !! WALL OF TEXT ALERT !! Okay, as you may know string theory postulates ten spacial dimensions: the three we know, six that are ridiculously tiny and 'curled up', and one that encompasses all the others. I have no friggin' clue how the math works (or doesn't), so don't ask. What I wonder, however, is why they stick to just one time dimension. Consider it this way: why can't every possible alternative state change from the quantum level up create a different possible future? As long as we keep moving forward it seems like that was the way it had to be, but that doesn't prove it absolutely had to be that way, does it? If we can't, what does that say about free will? Determinism, the concept that all actual outcomes are direct results of the overall state of the universe in previous times and thus other possibilities do not actually exist, answers in the negative. Determinism is rather important proposition for scientific experimental validity, so we don't want to toss that out to hastily... but it's also just based on past experience so it's only as valid as the data backing it up, thus not 100% certain. I'll come back to this in a bit. Okay, imagine that at any point in time some potentially infinite number of possible timelines extend forwards to different future states, each depending on a different combination of resulting values from all the various quantum uncertainties. Every possible future is a real place we choose to go to with every choice we make (based on quantum processes in our brains, or souls, or whatever does the actual thinking in us... science isn't 100% sure here yet). Funny thing about lines though... they extend in -two- directions. Applied to the concept of a timeline, this means there are multiple pasts leading to any given present. This, I propose, is how free will and determinism can both be correct: though the present spacial-state we experience is perfectly predictable from absolute knowledge of the past state, by switching to a different timeline one adopts not only a different future but also a different past! Consider the ramifications for this! Choosing a particular future outcome may actually change your past such that the real things you remember no longer actually happened in quite the same way as they do in your current timeline. Likewise, if one decides that some past event occurred differently than it actually did in the current timeline, it might be possible to shift to a new future that does accommodate this 'new' past. Astounding, no? That's nice and all, but I'm sure most of you are wondering what the hell any of this has to do with EVA. Well... here goes. Geometry tells us that any number of possible lines can pass through a single point, but there is only one specific line that can pass through any two given points. Thus, if someone were to suddenly gain the godlike ability to redefine reality to whatever situation he wanted (even if he didn't really 'get it' that he had this power), whatever his choice was would need to be defined by two given anchor points. Thus, Rei bookends. Discuss?

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sat Jan 24, 2009 11:30 pm Having only skimmed the wall of text, I may not be giving a good response, but... We only need one time dimension. It doesn't really matter which one. Let's say we were some race with the same three-dimensional viewpoint and one-dimensional time, but had time swapped with one of our existing dimensions. It would seem weird from our perspective, but is it any more innately weird? It's quite possible that the time dimension is arbitrary, and that all actions influence all of spacetime. If gravity can bend time, combine emergence and chaos theory with that and we can come up with a decent argument for every movement modifying everything all throughout time. Obviously, since we are stuck with moving across time and only seeing the three dimensions, and ourselves not being outside of all of it, we would never be able to notice how our own actions change the past. We are stuck in a deterministic point of view, even if we can change all of time with the swipe of our hand, because to us it would be as though nothing had changed at all.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Jan 25, 2009 2:50 am Something tells me I'm not the only one who's been hitting the Marathon lately.
